perturbation.original_text: 1.1 The following words shall have the following meanings throughout this Agreement: a) "Agreement" means this Agreement and all Schedules annexed hereto, as amended from time to time by the Parties; b) "Freight" means any goods directed to one Party by the other for pick up and/or delivery; c) "Force Majeure" means i) An Act of God; ii) A strike, lock out or other labour disturbance; 2 - 2 - iii) A war, revolution, insurrection, riot, blockade or any other unlawful act against public order authority; iv) A storm, fire, flood, explosion, lightening or other hazardous weather condition; v) Any Ministry of Transportation road closure or other acts of government or transport authorities which are beyond the control of the Parties; vi) Any air traffic control delays, cancellations, reroutes or other acts of government, airport or aviation authorities, which are beyond the control of the Parties; vii) Any loss, hijacking, government seizure or diversion.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 The following words shall have the following meanings throughout this Agreement: a) "Agreement" means this Agreement and all Schedules annexed hereto, as amended from time to time by the Parties; b) "Freight" means any goods directed to one Party by the other for pick up and/or delivery; c) "Force Majeure" means conditions beyond a parties control.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the detailed clauses that specifies what constitutes "Force Majeure", we create an in-text contradiction. Now, section 3.4 refers to delays caused by "events of Force Majeure," without clearly defining what that entails beyond 'conditions beyond a parties control'. This opens the door to interpretation, creating uncertainty in enforcement of the performance standards outlined in section 3.4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees to provide the services outlined above at an on time performance level of no less than ninety percent (90%) of the scheduled delivery time, excluding delays caused by the other Party or events of Force Majeure. Monthly, the performance level shall be measured as set out above. Failure to provide services as set out herein constitutes a Monthly Service Failure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of "Force Majeure" from a detailed list of events to a vague "conditions beyond a parties control". This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which excludes delays caused by "events of Force Majeure" from the on-time performance calculation. The original definition provided a clear scope for what constituted a Force Majeure event, whereas the changed text introduces ambiguity, making it difficult to determine whether a specific delay should be excluded from the performance calculation. This ambiguity creates a strong in-text contradiction because it undermines the enforceability of the performance standards. As per the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction: Omissions occur when essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections. Here essential language was removed.

perturbation.original_text: 5.5 Every invoice shall be reviewed by the invoiced Party and subject to paragraphs 5.2, shall pay the invoice net fifteen (15) days from the date of invoicing. Invoices shall be delivered to the invoiced Party within three (3) days of the date of invoicing. 6 - 6 - 5.6 Interest, at the prime rate then charged to commercial customers by the Canadian Imperil Bank of Commerce (Toronto Main Branch), shall be payable on all amounts overdue for thirty (30) days or more. 5.7 Any discrepancy in an invoice which is discovered by either Party may result in the issuance of a debit note or credit note by the appropriate Party, and notwithstanding any prior payment, the same shall be accompanied by supporting documentation. Payment shall be made by the appropriate Party net fifteen (15) days from receipt and acceptance of the documentation.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 Every invoice shall be reviewed by the invoiced Party and subject to paragraphs 5.2, shall pay the invoice net fifteen (15) days from the date of invoicing. Invoices shall be delivered to the invoiced Party within three (3) days of the date of invoicing. 5.7 Any discrepancy in an invoice which is discovered by either Party may result in the issuance of a debit note or credit note by the appropriate Party, and notwithstanding any prior payment, the same shall be accompanied by supporting documentation. Payment shall be made by the appropriate Party net fifteen (15) days from receipt and acceptance of the documentation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing section 5.6, which sets out the Interest rate, section 17.2(b) becomes contradictory. Now, a Party that is not in default can recover monies due and to become due, but the interest rate that accrues after 30 days is no longer defined. The contract is still reffering that interest is due, but it is not defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the occurrence of an Event of Default and in addition to any rights or remedies available to it under this Agreement or at law or in equity, the Party not in default may exercise any or all of the following remedies: (a) terminate this Agreement, upon giving one hundred and twenty (120) days written notice, otherwise upon written notice with respect to 17.1 (g) and (h); (b) recover from the defaulting Party any and all monies then due and to become due; and (c) take possession, immediately, without demand or notice, without any court order or other process of law, any and all of its property (including bags and containers) and Freight received by the defaulting Party under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The removal of section 5.6, which defined the interest rate applicable to overdue amounts, creates a contradiction with section 17.2(b). Section 17.2(b) allows the non-defaulting party to recover monies due and to become due, which implicitly includes interest on overdue amounts. However, without section 5.6, the contract no longer specifies the interest rate, making it unclear how much interest is owed and creating a direct conflict, as the obligation to pay interest exists (per 17.2(b)) but the mechanism to calculate it is missing (due to the omission of 5.6). This creates an inconsistency in the contract, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: 18.1 In the event a Party has, in any twelve (12) month period, more than three (3) Monthly Performance Failures, then the defaulting Party shall pay a penalty to the Party not in default, which the Parties acknowledge is a pre-estimation of damages suffered by the non-defaulting Party due to the current month's Monthly Performance Failure ("Default Month"). For each Default Month, the Party in default shall pay a penalty equal to five (5) times the Party not in default's corporate average yield during the Default Month for each shipment below the performance commitment. For example, if the defaulting Party, in a Default Month, provided services at an eighty five percent (85%) level and the average yield for the Default Month of the Party not in default is ten dollars ($10.00) and the total number of shipments handled by the defaulting Party pursuant to this Agreement is one hundred (100), then the penalty would be equal to 5 x[(Performance Commitment - Actual Performance Level) x # of shipments] x average yield or 5 x [(90-85) x 100] x 10 = $250.00. 100
perturbation.changed_text: 18.1 In the event a Party has, in any twelve (12) month period, more than three (3) Monthly Performance Failures, then the defaulting Party shall pay a penalty to the Party not in default, which the Parties acknowledge is a pre-estimation of damages suffered by the non-defaulting Party due to the current month's Monthly Performance Failure ("Default Month").
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of how to calculate the Penalty for a Default Month, we are creating an in-text contradiction. The contract still makes refrence to a penality that is due, but doesn't define how to calculate it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event a Party has, in any twelve (12) month period, more than three (3) Monthly Performance Failures, then the defaulting Party shall pay a penalty to the Party not in default, which the Parties acknowledge is a pre-estimation of damages suffered by the non-defaulting Party due to the current month's Monthly Performance Failure ("Default Month").
perturbation.justification: The original text defined how the penalty for a Default Month is calculated. The changed text removes this definition, while the contradicted text refers to the penalty. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contract references a penalty without specifying how to determine its amount, making the penalty clause unenforceable. The changed text breaks compatibility with the contradicted section by omitting essential language (the calculation method) that is necessary for the enforcement of the penalty outlined in the contradicted section, thus adhering to the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
